forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea will maintain a ridge across South Australia today. Northerly winds freshen today ahead of a front that is forecast to move across the far west this afternoon, then continue across the state during the weekend. The low pressure system associated with this front is expected to linger over waters to the south of the state resulting in a trough moving over southern parts later Sunday and Monday. The low will then move into Bass Strait on Monday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Northerly 15 to 25 knots.
1 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 25 knots turning northwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Northwesterly 25 to 30 knots turning westerly during the afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
West to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning northeasterly in the late afternoon. Winds reaching up to 25 knots offshore in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northeasterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore during the morning. Winds tending north to northwesterly 15 to 25 knots in the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres by early evening.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres during the afternoon, then increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
West to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning northeasterly in the evening. Winds reaching up to 25 knots offshore in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Mostly sunny.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northeasterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds tending north to northwesterly during the day.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending westerly 2.5 to 4 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.
